english: cheek (cf. kúr-tsós (khur tshos)), *ḍám-pa hom soṅ* ('dam pa hom song) W. his cheeks are fallen; lág-pa grám-pa-la rtén-pa (lag pa 'gram pa la rten pa) to lay one's hand on the cheek (in a pensive or sorrowful mood) Dzl. Comp. gram-lcág ('gram lcag) a slap on the face, box on the ear; *ḍam-cág gyáb-ce* ('dam lcag rgyab ce) W. to box a person's ears. — gram-ću ldaṅ ('gram chu ldang) that makes one's mouth water Sch. — gram-púg ('gram pug) Lt.? — *ḍam-dzóg* ('dam rdzog) C. a blow or cuff with the fist upon the cheek, *gyág-pa* (rgyag pa) to deal such blows. — gram-rús ('gram rus) cheek-bone, jaw-bone. — grám-ša ('gram sha) the flesh of the cheek. — gram-yšóg ('gram gshog) the hinder part of the jaw-bone Sch. — grám-so ('gram so) cheek-tooth, molar-tooth, grinder.
to hurt Lt.; of wounds: to get inflamed, rma (rma) f. Mil. nt.
a disease, fever in consequence of great exertions Med.
to hate, to bear ill-will, to have a spite against, *ṅa ḱó-la źe ḍé dug* (nga kho la zhe de dug) C. I hate him in my heart.
1. gelatine, jelly of meat C. 2. v. the following article.

(cf. sgríg-pa (sgrig pa)) to suit, agree, correspond, to be right, adequate, sufficient, in B. seldom, col. very frq., *ḍíg-pa yin* ('dig pa yin) C. that will do, I am satisfied; *da ḍíg* (da 'dig) W. now that will do! just enough now! *ḍíg-ḍíg* ('dig 'dig) W. to be sure! quite so! of course! *o ḍíg gog* (o 'dig gog) W. yes, to be sure! *tsỏ-će mi ḍíg* (tsho ce mi 'dig) W. it is not yet time for cooking; *tó-re tság-na ḍíg-ga* (tho re tshag na 'dig ga) W. will it be early enough, if I sift it to-morrow? *de yaṅ mi ḍíg-pa dug* (de yang mi 'dig pa dug) W. also that is not practicable; ṅa ǰọ́-yaṅ di ma ḍíg-na (nga jo yang di ma 'dig na) if my pronunciation is not correct C. (Lewin).

pf. gríb ('grib), 1. to grow less, to decrease, to be diminished, syn to °brí-ba ('bri ba); mi grib mi lúd-pa (mi 'grib mi lud pa) neither to grow less nor to flow over Dzl.; but gen. °pél-ba ('phel ba) is opposed to gríb-pa ('grib pa), and both words refer not only to bulk, size, and quantity, but also to strength, well-being etc., so that gríb-pa ('grib pa) also means to sink, decay, be reduced; bskál-pa mar grib (bskal pa mar 'grib), acc. to Schr. = Treta yuga v. dus (dus) 6; mar gríb-pa (mar 'grib pa) also opp. to yar skyé-ba (yar skye ba) to be re-born in lower regions. — 2. to grow dim, to get dark, cf. sgríb-pa (sgrib pa) Cs.
in lag-grím (lag 'grim) Glr. 45: lag-grím-gyis brgyus-pas (lag 'grim gyis brgyus pas) passing from hand to hand, v. grím-pa ('grim pa) II, 1.

I. sometimes for °brím-pa ('brim pa) Pth. II. pf. °gríms ('grims) 1. to go, walk, march about, perambulate, to rove or stroll idling about, rgyal-kams (rgyal khams) over the countries Mil.; yćig-pur rí-kŕód-la (gcig pur ri khrod la) Mil.; bár-dor (bar dor) in the bardo (q.v.) Thgr.; mi-sér ǰág-pai grím-sa yin (mi ser jag pa'i 'grim sa yin) it is a resort of robbers Mil.; it is also used of the course of the veins in the body Med. — 2. W. to go off, to sell, to meet with a ready sale. — 3. ríg-pa grím-pa (rig pa 'grim pa) v. ríg-pa (rig pa).

pf. gríl ('gril) (cf. sgríl-ba (sgril ba)) 1. to be twisted or wrapped round, Dzl. MS, 17. acc. to one manuscript, for kŕí-ba (dkri ba) Sch.; to be collected, concentrated, to flock or crowd together, kun gríl-nas (kun 'gril nas) all in a heap, all together Mil. — 2. to be turned, rounded, made circular or cylindric, e.g. a stick Mil. — 3. to fall, e.g. leaves from a tree; in B. seldom, in W. frq. (°dríl-ba ('dril ba) is the same word).
pf. grus ('grus), to bestow pains upon a thing, slob-pa-la (slob pa la) upon study Dzl.

pf. grub ('grub) 1. to be made ready, to be finished, accomplished; grúb-pa mi srid ('grub pa mi srid) it is not possible that this be accomplished Glr.; ma °grúb-par (ma 'grub par) before its having been finished Glr.; ma-grúb-pa-rnams grúb-par °gyur-ro (ma 'grub pa rnams 'grub par 'gyur ro) (frq. of charms, regarding their desired effect) prob. means: all that has not yet been effected, will be accomplished by it; grúb-pa-rnams ('grub pa rnams) is applied in a special sense to the ordained Gelongs (v. dge-slóṅ (dge slong)); šúgs-la grub (shugs la 'grub) the
